full_response:
Known data  
 Rest-frame (proper) lifetime of the muon: τμ = 1.0×10⁻⁶ s  
 Height of atmosphere layer: L = 30 000 m  
 Speed for both downward and upward trips (measured in the Earth frame):  
  v = 0.99999 c , β ≡ v/c = 0.99999  

1. Lorentz factor  
 γ = 1/√(1−β²)  
 β² = 0.99999² ≈ 0.99998 → 1−β² = 2.0×10⁻⁵  
 γ = 1/√(2.0×10⁻⁵) ≈ 1/0.0044721 ≈ 2.236×10² ≈ 2.24×10²

2. Downward trip (Earth frame)  
 Time in Earth frame: t₁ = L / v  
  t₁ = 30 000 m / (0.99999·3.00×10⁸ m s⁻¹) ≈ 1.0000×10⁻⁴ s = 100 µs  

 Proper time experienced by muon on the way down:  
  τ₁ = t₁ / γ = (1.0000×10⁻⁴ s)/(2.236×10²) ≈ 4.47×10⁻⁷ s = 0.447 µs

3. Upward trip  
 Identical to the downward trip → τ₃ = τ₁ = 4.47×10⁻⁷ s

4. Holding time at rest on the ground  
 While the muon is stopped its proper time equals the laboratory time: τ_hold = t_hold.  
 Demand that the total proper time equal the muon’s lifetime:

  τ_total = τ₁ + τ_hold + τ₃ = τμ  
  ⇒ τ_hold = τμ − 2τ₁
  ⇒ τ_hold = 1.0×10⁻⁶ s − 2(4.47×10⁻⁷ s)  
  ⇒ τ_hold ≈ 1.06×10⁻⁷ s = 0.106 µs = 1.1×10⁻⁷ s

Thus the muon must be kept on the surface only about 10⁻⁷ seconds (≈100 ns) before being re-launched upward if it is to decay exactly when it reaches the 30 km level again.

\boxed{t_{\text{hold}}\;\approx\;1\times 10^{-7}\ \text{s}\; \text{(about }0.1\ \mu\text{s)}}  

Confidence: 88%
